rationale: The citing paper references Berger and Milkman (2012) only in grouped literature-list citations to characterize prior marketing work on why people share online content. It does not discuss that paper's specific findings, mechanisms, or measures in developing hypotheses, designing analyses, or interpreting results. The core theory and mechanism in the citing paper (idea vs. identity sharing and elaboration in MOOCs) are built from other sources, so this citation functions as broad background positioning.
